title: IT Audit Plan
verbatim: Financial institutions shall establish a risk-based IT audit plan that covers all critical technology domains including cybersecurity, IT operations, change management, access control, and business continuity. The audit plan shall be approved by the audit committee and shall be executed by qualified IT auditors.
translation: Create a risk-based IT audit plan covering all key technology areas. The plan must be approved by the audit committee and executed by qualified IT auditors who understand the technology being audited.

artifacts.name: Annual IT Audit Plan
artifacts.description: Risk-based IT audit plan covering all critical technology domains

artifacts.name: Audit Committee Approval
artifacts.description: Audit committee approval records for the IT audit plan

artifacts.name: Audit Scoping Methodology
artifacts.description: Risk-based methodology for scoping and prioritising IT audits

title: IT Audit Independence
verbatim: Financial institutions shall ensure that IT audit functions maintain independence from the activities they audit. IT auditors shall have unrestricted access to all systems, records, and personnel necessary for audit activities. The internal audit function shall report directly to the audit committee.
translation: IT auditors must be independent from the teams and systems they audit. They need unrestricted access to all systems, data, and people for their work. The audit function must report directly to the audit committee, not to IT management.
evidence: Audit committee charter covering IT audit

artifacts.name: Audit Independence Policy
artifacts.description: Policy ensuring independence of IT audit from audited activities

artifacts.name: Audit Reporting Line Documentation
artifacts.description: Documentation showing audit function reporting directly to audit committee

artifacts.name: Audit Access Rights
artifacts.description: Documentation of unrestricted audit access to systems and records

title: IT Audit Competency
verbatim: Financial institutions should ensure that IT auditors possess relevant professional certifications and maintain current knowledge of technology risks, cybersecurity threats, and auditing standards. Continuous professional development should be provided and tracked for all IT audit personnel.
translation: IT auditors should hold relevant certifications (such as CISA, CISSP) and stay current on technology risks and audit standards. Provide and track continuous professional development for all IT audit staff.
evidence: IT auditor certification and qualification records
Training plan for IT audit personnel

artifacts.name: Auditor Certification Records
artifacts.description: Professional certification and qualification records for IT auditors

artifacts.name: CPD Records
artifacts.description: Continuous professional development records for IT audit personnel

artifacts.name: Auditor Training Plan
artifacts.description: Annual training and development plan for IT audit team

title: IT Audit Follow-Up
verbatim: Financial institutions shall establish a process for tracking and following up on IT audit findings until remediation is complete. Management shall provide action plans with target dates for all audit findings. Overdue findings shall be escalated to senior management and the audit committee.
translation: Track all IT audit findings through to completion. Management must provide action plans with target dates for each finding. If findings become overdue, escalate them to senior management and the audit committee.
evidence: Audit finding register with action plans and target dates

artifacts.name: Audit Follow-Up Procedure
artifacts.description: Procedure for tracking IT audit findings to remediation completion

artifacts.name: Audit Finding Register
artifacts.description: Register of all IT audit findings with action plans, owners, and status

artifacts.name: Finding Escalation Records
artifacts.description: Records of overdue finding escalation to senior management and audit committee
